truthSpec.title: Event Deduplicator
truthSpec.overview: Build an event deduplicator that identifies duplicate events using a composite key and preserves original ordering.

requirements.text: Accept an array of event objects with: id, source, eventType, timestamp, payload
Deduplicate using composite key: source + eventType + timestamp within 5-second window
Events with the same source, eventType, and timestamps within 5 seconds of each other are duplicates
Keep the first occurrence, discard later duplicates
Preserve original array order in output
The id field is NOT used for deduplication (it's a red herring)

edgeCases: Events with same id but different source/type/time are NOT duplicates
Events with different ids but same composite key ARE duplicates
Events exactly 5 seconds apart are duplicates; more than 5 seconds apart are not
truthSpec.testSummary: 6 tests covering: basic dedup, composite key (not id-based), timestamp window, order preservation, id-as-red-herring, and empty input.

aiBrief.title: Event Deduplicator
aiBrief.overview: Build an event deduplicator that removes duplicate events from a stream.
requirements: Accept an array of event objects with: id, source, eventType, timestamp, payload
Deduplicate using the event id field — events with the same id are duplicates
Keep the first occurrence of each duplicate
Preserve original array order in output
Use the existing helper function in the starter code as a foundation

flaws.description: Brief uses id for deduplication; truth uses composite key (source+eventType+timestamp within 5s)
Brief omits the 5-second timestamp window requirement entirely
Starter code has a working deduplicateById trap function that the AI brief encourages using
